title: neottopteris squamulata
intro: Neottopteris squamulata, also known as the shield fern or shield-leafed water fern, is a species of water fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This aquatic plant is native to Southeast Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues. The leaves and roots of Neottopteris squamulata have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and prevent the spread of infections. Herbal preparations of Neottopteris squamulata include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems.
